title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
notes:
Continuous micro-stuttering. Most noticeable when looking up/down, it appears that the frames are rendered out of order, e.g. 1,3,2,4,6,5,7 etc...
When encountering a shader for the first time, the initial quick compile will often generate a very white shader, and a few seconds later it is fine.
The shader (probably compile) issue reduces significantly after playing a few minutes, and doesn't repeat when re-starting the game.
Framerate is however excellent.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
notes: Stable gameplay. Keyboard problems (Caps lock toggle run/walk problem, moving direction problems), can't save, can't change options, and can't access addons option.

notes: When creating the wine prefix, proton uses the system default language, french in my case, but i always use steam in english, so the game didn't find its 'My documents' folder because it was translated in french. Having proton creating the wine prefix with steam language instead of system, or always adding english versions of such userdirs would cure it definitly, i think

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes:
Started a new save and played about an hour until I reached Whiterun. I used the Unofficial Skyrim Legendary Edition Patch from the workshop. Particle effects (fire, basically) seems to make the game struggle FPS wise (not a steady 60) and cause quite a bit of input lag for some reason. Since the beginning of the game has an unusually large amount of fire, performance didn't look great.  However, during my hour of play I didnt' encounter other areas with these issues.

Pressing caps lock to toggle between run and walk doesn't always register, or register unreliably. The game hangs on exit which is a fairly old bug and apparently also occurs on Windows, though rarely.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Framerates over 60 mess with the engine, used an FPS limiter mod to fix physics bugs. This was also slightly buggy though, so I set my monitor refresh rate to 60hz and that fixed a few things. Other than that, the mouse is slightly wonky and difficult to control

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Runs pretty great overall @ 1080p Ultra Settings (using FXAA) with all DLC / no mods installed. Didn't run entirely ootb and still has a few annoyances: Cloud Sync of Saves only worked partially; Launcher throws error messages, though inconsequential; Playing with Steam Controller in Keyboard Mode doesn't allow remapping in game (Recognizes all keys as "0x45"); Quitting the game always causes a freeze; No sound crackling issues for me, lip sync can be off at times though.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Doesn't start if you click Play after the error message on the launcher. Click it before and it's fine. Many glitches when going through doors. Horrendous physics glitches e.g. items flying around, you falling through bridges. This I believe is an FPS related problem as it happens on Windows. I got 144FPS, but I didn't make any changes to allow it. Game still hangs on exit. I needed to reboot my PC. I think 3.7-8 is more stable than 3.16-3. Playable but annoying.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Stable framerates (lower than windows) but otherwise perfect
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Running the "Legendary Edition" (all DLC, but not the "Special" edition).  Everything works out-of-the-box.  I use an XBox clone controller mostly but Keyboard works fine, too.  No issue with sound or graphics at all.  Because physical constraints limit me to a GT 1030, at 1080p I default to "HIgh" settings but dial-down anti-aliasing to 4x (I honestly can't see the difference) for a decent frame-rate boost to approx 60 fps average.  It has the same foible as under Windows in that it's best to quit to the main menu, alt-tab to a terminal and issue "pkill TESV" unless you disable (deselect) Dawnguard DLC which then allows you to quit normally to desktop.  Personally I'd prefer to retain the DLC and live with this as a minor irritation.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Everything seems to work, even have an ENB and oodles of mods, but struck with the "64hz bug" with, as on Windows, no fix.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Steam Play is amazing, it runs perfectly, even the joystick, if you have some audio issues set PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=30 %command% in the launch options.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: I'm playing without any expansion packs or mods & haven't done much testing due to the main issue I'm running into. Due to my older system, I have graphics set on their lowest, but the updated graphics card still lets me play at around 40-50FPS, which is about the same as what I get in Windows. Anyway, my issue is that movement of any kind gets extremely jittery as the game loads new textures & stuff. It smooths out once they're all loaded, but this is one thing that didn't happen during my playtime in Windows. As such, I can't recommend playing this game on anything but the fastest systems until the caching issues are figured out.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: For nVidia users like myself experiencing stuttering issues & jerky movement, it's due to nVidia's drivers limiting the shader cache to 128MB. Adding "export __GL_SHADER_DISK_CACHE_SKIP_CLEANUP=1" to my ~/.profile cleared this up real quick. Performance is on par with its Windows counterpart. I have noticed the launcher remaining behind afterwards like other people, but as stated, it's easy enough to kill via the task manager.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Runs fairly well out of the box, albeit with a slight performance hit compared to Windows. The main issue is freezing on quit to desktop, which requires manually killing the process. Mods work just fine if manually installed, including the script extender. Borderless windowed mode is highly recommended, as it both improves performance and makes forcing the window to close after quitting the game to be a bit more convenient.

Skyrim SE had more problems.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Crashes in the main menu as soon as you press a key or move the mouse cursor over one of the menu buttons. Possibly a bug related to the new faudio system as I used to be able to load into the gameplay about a month ago, but back then NPC audio didn't work and it would randomly crash.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Found and used old character from playing on Windows, then started new character. Tried playing using Wine earlier with many (mostly input related) problems, but using Proton has so far been without hitches. Playing on highest settings.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: It has only crashed once in 100 hours, that's a better crash rate than I had on Windows. Currently running 23 mods as well as the script extender without issue.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Did not need to add any launch options. Game runs perfectly. Just install and press play. On the rare occassion there might be a crackling of sound or some drop in frame rate for a few seconds. Did not add any mods.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Runs perfectly out of the box; tried mods and SKSE, and everything worked just as expected.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Minor lags sometimes.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: No issues. Runs perfectly on ultra.
title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

notes: Game itself runs perfectly out of the box. ENB requires all d3dx9 and d3dcompiler dlls through winetricks to get fully working, as well as d3d9 set to "native, builtin" in winecfg. skse_loader.exe must be renamed to SkyrimLauncher.exe to launch through Steam. Mod Organizer's virtual filesystem doesn't work, but files can be easily symlinked with movfs4l.py instead.

notes: Hangs on shutdown.

notes: Playable 1080@60FPS Crashes/Freeze upon exit (recommend to play in windowed mode)

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
notes:
Continuous micro-stuttering. Most noticeable when looking up/down, it appears that the frames are rendered out of order, e.g. 1,3,2,4,6,5,7 etc...
When encountering a shader for the first time, the initial quick compile will often generate a very white shader, and a few seconds later it is fine.
The shader (probably compile) issue reduces significantly after playing a few minutes, and doesn't repeat when re-starting the game.
Framerate is however excellent.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
notes: Stable gameplay. Keyboard problems (Caps lock toggle run/walk problem, moving direction problems), can't save, can't change options, and can't access addons option.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
notes: Only played the beginning, and that was completely stable.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
notes: After a few seconds the mouse stops to work. It's only possible to move the mouse if a mouse key is pressed down (right or left). Restarted a couple of times, error still exists. I have the same problem with S.T.A.L.K.E.R.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
notes: Does not work with keyboard/mouse. I had to attach Steam Controller and setup it as XBox Controller (setting bGamepadEnable=0 in SkyrimPrefs.ini didn't work). BTW: config file is deep in "SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/72850/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Skyrim/" directory.

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
notes:
The missing/crackling audio can easily be fixed by the linked guide. Not as tedious as it may seem! On a sidenote: in my case, setting xaudio2_6 to native and xaudio2_7 to builtin did the trick, and the Windows version is set to 8.1. No issues whatsoever, excellent performance.
https://www.reddit.com/r/linux_gaming/comments/99i4se/skyrim_on_linux_steam_play_no_voiceno_music_audio/

title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
notes: Sound a little distorted (lang. PL), crashed on exit - need manualy kill the proces. Graphics is perfect (game + all dlc + about 50 mods): 1920 x 1080, max detail, 60 fps.
